featured.title: Nocturne — Fall/Winter 2026
featured.excerpt: An exploration of shadow and structure. Twelve looks that move between architectural tailoring and fluid draping, rendered in a palette of midnight, charcoal, and burnt sienna.

featured.title: Jonathan Anderson's Dior Redefines Heritage
featured.excerpt: The Bar jacket, loosened and reinterpreted as pastel knits with fluted peplums — a runway skimming across a lily-pad pond in the Tuileries.

featured.title: Piccioli's Balenciaga: Clair-Obscur
featured.excerpt: Light and darkness collide in cocoon silhouettes, velvet gowns, and elevated denim.

featured.title: Red Thread
featured.excerpt: Following a single red thread from first sketch to runway.

stream.title: Sarah Burton's Givenchy Finds Its Voice in Third Outing
stream.excerpt: Not proposing one ideal woman but many women and many ways of being strong. Exact tailoring, power suits, draped detachable sleeves, and a leopard-print sequin dress with fringes signaled the collection where Burton's Givenchy point of view finally clicked. Stephen Jones designed white milkmaid headpieces that anchored every exit.

items.title: Loewe's McCollough & Hernandez Push Material Innovation to Its Limits
items.excerpt: 3D-printed latex slip dresses, inflatable parkas, and rubber-molded trainers — luxury craftsmanship reimagined with whimsy. Cosima von Bonin's blue velvet stuffed animals set the tone. The palette screamed in lurid yellows and highlighter greens.

items.title: Celine's Michael Rider Designs for the Everyday Parisian
items.excerpt: "Bienvenue chez Celine." The starting point for fall was a daily commute in Paris — loose trench coats over sequined dresses, navy coats with knotted leather belts, and knit dresses in abstract floral prints. Less accessorized, more streamlined.

stream.text: Fashion has to do with ideas, the way we live, what is happening.

stream.title: Hermès Conjures a Liminal Realm Between Day and Evening, Earth and Space
stream.excerpt: Nadège Vanhée's A/W 2026 collection moved through shimmering silk twill, leather cycling shorts, and shearling bombers in dark blues and deep russets. Visible zips sliced across the chest and down the length of dresses — function as adornment. A silk scarf worn as a cape was the season's most photographed moment.

items.title: Jean Paul Gaultier by Duran Lantink Channels Marlene Dietrich
items.excerpt: Pinstriped suits, pleated black gowns, and Fair Isle knit bodysuits in a 43-look lineup. Hat brims attached to jackets. Vintage glamour rebuilt for 2026.

items.title: Alexander McQueen: Seán McGirr's Romantic Darkness
items.excerpt: Floral wallpaper patterns meet Gothic silhouettes, delicate lace, and chain-mail knitwear. Models emerged through a maze of sheer white curtains. Bouncy ruffled hems softened the edge.

stream.text: 9 of the 15 largest luxury houses appointed new creative directors in the past 12 months.
stream.source: Business of Fashion — State of Fashion Report 2026

stream.title: Stars Hit Paris Runways, But Fall's Real Trend Was Dressing for Hard Times
stream.excerpt: Across Paris Fashion Week, designers presented clothes that work in real life, not just on Instagram. From Celine's commuter-chic to Givenchy's power suiting, the Fall 2026 season marked a decisive pivot toward pragmatism. With 46% of executives expecting conditions to worsen and tariffs looming, even the most elevated houses are designing for the world as it is.

items.title: Luxury Fashion Rethinks Its Value After Years of Price Hikes
items.excerpt: After raising prices with no bump in quality or creativity, the industry is being forced to rebuild trust with shoppers. Value brands are reducing lowest-priced SKUs by 15–25%, racing upmarket as the middle hollows out.

items.title: Burberry Celebrates 170 Years With Iconic Trench Portraits
items.excerpt: Black-and-white portraits featuring Kate Moss, Daisy Edgar-Jones, Teyana Taylor, and Kid Cudi. 'The Trench, Portraits of an Icon' campaign cements the house's most enduring symbol.

stream.text: The most interesting clothes are the ones that show their making.

items.title: Saks Global Files for Bankruptcy Owing Billions to Fashion's Biggest Names
items.excerpt: Parent of Saks Fifth Avenue, Neiman Marcus, and Bergdorf Goodman filed Chapter 11 after missing a $100 million debt payment, listing $3.4 billion in debts including $136 million owed to Chanel alone.

items.title: Demna's "Primavera" Gucci Debut Divides Critics
items.excerpt: Ultra-minimal seamless garments cut close to the body, inspired by Botticelli. Kate Moss closed the show in the brand's iconic GG logo thong.

stream.title: Maria Grazia Chiuri Makes Her Fendi Debut With "Less I, More Us"
stream.excerpt: The Italian designer presented her first collection as Fendi creative director at Milan Fashion Week, showing co-ed looks dominated by black with fluid satin, sheer gowns, and statement coats. Chiuri, who began her career at Fendi in 1989, signaled a shift toward pragmatism and collective emotion.

studio_content.title: Midnight Wool
studio_content.excerpt: On dead stock fabric, rust stains, designing at 1 AM, and the conversation between cloth and the person inside it.

studio_content.title: Raw Material: The Case for Imperfect Fashion
studio_content.excerpt: A long-form essay on why the most interesting clothes are the ones that show their making — raw edges, visible seams, and unfinished hems as a design philosophy.

studio_content.title: Soft Architecture — S/S 2026
studio_content.excerpt: Structured forms in flowing fabrics. A contradiction made wearable.

studio_content.title: Red Thread
studio_content.excerpt: Following a single red thread from first sketch to final stitch to runway to street.

personal_quote: Creating is the most honest thing I know how to do.
